original: CERN achieves first proton-antiproton beam collision (570 GeV)
Miguel Vasquez makes first public quadruple somersault on trapeze
FIFA Women's World Cup Final, Rose Bowl, Pasadena, CA: US beats China 5-4 on penalties; 0-0 a.e.t; US wins second World Cup
NASA's Juno spacecraft makes closest-ever pass over Jupiter's Great Red Spot at 9,000 kilometers overhead
The final four boys and their coach are rescued from Tham Luang Nang Non cave, Thailand, after being trapped there for 18 days by monsoon flooding
Willie Jones hospitalized for heatstroke from record 46.5°C temperature
Alexandra Palace in London burnt down for a second time
H Kamerlingh Onnes makes helium liquid (-269°C)
Original sketch of Winnie the Pooh's Hundred Acre Wood by E.H. Shepard sells for £430,000 in London, a record price for a book illustration
Bobby Lowe, American baseball second baseman (first player to hit 4 HRs in one MLB game, 1894 Boston Beaneaters), born in Pittsburgh, Pennsylvania (d. 1951)
Don Herbert, American scientist and TV host (Watch Mr Wizard), born in Waconia, Minnesota (d. 2007)
Eunice Kennedy Shriver, sister of President John F. Kennedy, founder of the Special Olympics, born in Brookline, Massachusetts (d. 2009)
Pittsburgh Pirates outslug Philadelphia Phillies 15-9 at Baker Bowl; 9 HRs hit, 1 in each inning - unique in MLB history
World's official highest recorded temperature of 134°F (56.7°C) occurs at Greenland Ranch (now known as Furnace Creek Ranch) in Death Valley, California
Telstar, first active communications satellite developed by American Telephone and Telegraph Company (AT&T), is launched
Bahamas declares independence from the United Kingdom and adopts constitution
Paula Ivan runs female world record 1 mile (4:15.61)
Spaceship Giotto (Halley 1986) approaches comet Grigg-Skjellerup
Yobes Ondieki runs world record 10 km (26:58.38)
Earliest evidence of modern humans outside Africa is found with 210,000-year-old skull from Apidima Cave, southern Greece, published in "Nature"
Carl Friedrich Gaussdiscovers that every positive integer is representable as a sum of at most three triangular numbers

Karl Plagge, German officer and Nazi Party member who during World War II used his position as a staff officer in the German Army to employ and protect some 1,240 Jews, born in Darmstadt, Germany (d. 1957)
Mildred Benson (née Augustine), American journalist and children's literature and mystery novelist (Nancy Drew; Penny Parker), born in Ladora, Iowa (d. 2002)
Joe Shuster, Canadian-American cartoonist (co-creator, with Jerry Siegel, of "Superman"), born in Toronto, Ontario (d. 1992)
Harvey Ball, American inventor and designer of popular 'smiley-face' graphic, born in Worcester, Massachusetts (d. 2001)
Mavis Staples, American gospel singer (The Staple Singers - "I'll Take You There"), born in Chicago, Illinois
Mary McLeod Bethune, American educator (Bethune-Cookman College) and civil rights leader, born in Maysville, South Carolina (d. 1955)
105°F (41°C) at North Bridgton, Maine (state record)
Uruguay becomes a member of the Berne Convention for the Protection of Literary and Artistic Works
Walt Disney's "Fox & The Hound" released
Frank Zybach, American farmer and inventor of center-pivot irrigation, US's most widely used irrigation system, born in  Lafayette, Oregon (d. 1980)[1]
Rolling Stones score their first US number 1 single "(I Can't Get No) Satisfaction"
Scotland'sBonnie Prince Charlieflees in disguise to Isle of Skye
Urbain Le Verrier and John Couch Adams, co-discoverers of Neptune, meet for first time at home ofJohn Herschel
Wyoming becomes 44th state of US, the first with female suffrage
Dutch parliament approves women's right to vote
Two-pound hailstones kill 23 people and many cattle in Rostov, Russia
111°F (44°C) Phoenixville, Pennsylvania (state record)
"Yankee Clipper" completes first passenger flight over Atlantic
The Beatles release "A Hard Day's Night", their third studio album
Rangers' Larry Parrish hits his third grand slam of the week
TheComte de Rochambeauand his French force of 7,000 land in Newport, Rhode Island, to join the American Revolutionary War
Belgian-Romani-French jazz guitaristDjango Reinhardt's "Quintette du Hot Club" debuts at La Grosse Pomme nightclub in Montmartre, Paris
Jesse Bennett, American physician who performed 1st successful US Caesarean (1794), born in Frankford, Philadelphia, Pennsylvania (d. 1842)
Solomon Northup, American abolitionist and author (12 Years a Slave), born in Minerva, New York (or 10 Jul 1807) (d. 1863)
Alvan Graham Clark, American telescope maker and astronomer (Sirius B), born in Fall River, Massachusetts (d. 1897)
Herb McKenley, Jamaican athlete (Olympic gold 4 x 400m relay 1952; 3 x silver 1948, 52), born in Pleasant Valley, Clarendon, Jamaica (d. 2007)
Earl Hamner Jr., American creator and narrator of TV show "The Waltons", born in Schuyler, Virginia (d. 2016)
John Bradley, United States Navy corpsman, one of six who raised flag on top of Mt. Suribachi (see Iwo Jima), born in Antigo, Wisconsin (d. 1994)
Pyotr Ilyich Klimuk, Polish cosmonaut (Soyuz 13, 18B, 30), born in Komarovka, Belarus
Ronnie James Dio [Padavona], American singer, songwriter and musician (Elf; Rainbow; Black Sabbath; Dio; and Heaven & Hell), born in Portsmouth, New Hampshire (d. 2010)
[Sixto] Rodríguez, American singer-songwriter and guitarist ("Sugar Man"), born in Detroit, Michigan (d. 2023)

110°F (43°C) at Runyon, New Jersey (state record)
112°F (44°C) at Martinsburg, West Virginia (state record)

Hurricane Dennis slams into the Florida Panhandle causing billions of dollars in damage
